ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "incident_number" TEXT /* Incident number assigned by the police department */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the incident */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "day_of_week" TEXT /* Day of the week the incident occurred */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date and time the incident was reported */,
  "incident_time" TEXT /* Time the incident occurred */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the police district where the incident occurred */,
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the resolution of the incident */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address where the incident occurred */,
  "x_coordinate" REAL /* X-coordinate of the incident location */,
  "y_coordinate" REAL /* Y-coordinate of the incident location */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographic location of the incident in latitude and longitude format */,
  "reporting_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reported the incident */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the incident (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the incident (e.g., open, in progress, closed) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the incident was updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_reporting_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("reporting_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_resolution_id FOREIGN KEY ("resolution_id") REFERENCES resolutions ("resolution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., LARCENY/THEFT, DRUG/NARCOTIC) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent category (for hierarchical categories) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district (e.g., CENTRAL, MISSION) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the district */,
  "headquarter_address" TEXT /* Address of the district's headquarter */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the district */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address for the district */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resolutions" (
  "resolution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resolution */,
  "resolution_name" TEXT /* Name of the resolution (e.g., NONE, ARREST, BOOKED) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the resolution */,
  "is_final" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the resolution is final or if the case is still open */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("resolution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., officer, investigator, admin) */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged into the system */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being logged */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the log entry */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time the log entry was created */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log entry (e.g., update, resolution, note) */,
  "log_description" TEXT /* Description of the log entry */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "is_important" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the log entry is important */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_logs_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the file belongs to */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of file (e.g., report, evidence, photo) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_hash" TEXT /* SHA-256 hash of the file for integrity verification */,
  "file_extension" TEXT /* File extension for easier management */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_files_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_notes" (
  "note_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each note */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the note belongs to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the note */,
  "note_date" TEXT /* Date and time the note was created */,
  "note_text" TEXT /* Text of the note */,
  "is_confidential" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the note is confidential */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("note_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_notes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_notes_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_suspects" (
  "suspect_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each suspect */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the suspect is involved in */,
  "suspect_name" TEXT /* Full name of the suspect */,
  "suspect_age" INTEGER /* Age of the suspect */,
  "suspect_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the suspect */,
  "suspect_description" TEXT /* Description of the suspect (e.g., height, weight, identifying marks) */,
  "suspect_contact" TEXT /* Contact information of the suspect */,
  "suspect_address" TEXT /* Address of the suspect */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("suspect_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_suspects_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_victims" (
  "victim_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each victim */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident the victim is involved in */,
  "victim_name" TEXT /* Full name of the victim */,
  "victim_age" INTEGER /* Age of the victim */,
  "victim_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the victim */,
  "victim_description" TEXT /* Description of the victim (e.g., height, weight, identifying marks) */,
  "victim_contact" TEXT /* Contact information of the victim */,
  "victim_address" TEXT /* Address of the victim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("victim_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_victims_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)
